title: [BUG] Missing `indexed` attribute in the color object.
author: StephenChips
body:
## 🐛 Bug Report
There is an `indexed` attribute in the color object. When you set a cell's background color with color in the default color palette, Excel will probably add an `indexed` attribute to the `fill.color` object, instead of an `argb` attribute. It seems that this situation is quite common, for colors in the default color palette are used oftenly.

I did a little bit research on this attribute, and found a document that explained what these `indexed`'s value represent:
[https://openpyxl.readthedocs.io/en/stable/styles.html](https://openpyxl.readthedocs.io/en/stable/styles.html). Just open the webpage and `Ctrl+F` search for `indexed`, and you will get the result.

I think it is better to have an converting function to convert the `indexed` and `theme` attribute to an `arbg` color code, for when handling `.xlsx` document, the color code is what I really want to get.

comments.author: gone369
comments.body:
Incase someone needs the colors mapped out: 
```js
const indexedColors = [
  '000000',
  'FFFFFF',
  'FF0000',
  '00FF00',
  '0000FF',
  'FFFF00',
  'FF00FF',
  '00FFFF',
  '000000',
  'FFFFFF',
  'FF0000',
  '00FF00',
  '0000FF',
  'FFFF00',
  'FF00FF',
  '00FFFF',
  '800000',
  '008000',
  '000080',
  '808000',
  '800080',
  '008080',
  'C0C0C0',
  '808080',
  '9999FF',
  '993366',
  'FFFFCC',
  'CCFFFF',
  '660066',
  'FF8080',
  '0066CC',
  'CCCCFF',
  '000080',
  'FF00FF',
  'FFFF00',
  '00FFFF',
  '800080',
  '800000',
  '008080',
  '0000FF',
  '00CCFF',
  'CCFFFF',
  'CCFFCC',
  'FFFF99',
  '99CCFF',
  'FF99CC',
  'CC99FF',
  'FFCC99',
  '3366FF',
  '33CCCC',
  '99CC00',
  'FFCC00',
  'FF9900',
  'FF6600',
  '666699',
  '969696',
  '003366',
  '339966',
  '003300',
  '333300',
  '993300',
  '993366',
  '333399',
  '333333',
];
```

For tinting, you can probably refer to this stackoverflow answer: 
[https://stackoverflow.com/questions/5560248/programmatically-lighten-or-darken-a-hex-color-or-rgb-and-blend-colors](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/5560248/programmatically-lighten-or-darken-a-hex-color-or-rgb-and-blend-colors)
